issues.issue: Test has weak assertion that doesn't verify AC-5 requirement
issues.details: Test only checks `g.Expect(q).NotTo(BeNil())` which always passes for any struct. AC-5 requires 'Question text references gathered context where relevant', but this test doesn't verify that context is actually referenced or included.
issues.suggested_fix: Test should verify that when relevant context exists in `gathered` map, the InterviewQuestion.Context field is populated OR the question text includes context references. For example, check that when 'related-tech' context mentions SQLite, the tech-stack question's Context field contains that information.

strengths: All 6 acceptance criteria have corresponding tests
Tests fail for correct reason: stub returns empty list, not compilation/import errors
9/10 tests fail as expected in red phase
1 test (all answered → empty) correctly passes with stub behavior
Tests use appropriate gomega matchers for readability
Tests are blackbox: testing public SelectQuestions API
Good coverage of gap size tiers: small (85%), medium (60-65%), large (20-30%)
Priority ordering tested across multiple scenarios
Edge cases covered: all answered, no critical unanswered, all priorities
Test data is realistic with proper priority distributions

checklist.item: Each acceptance criterion has at least one test
checklist.details: AC-1: 2 tests, AC-2: 3 tests, AC-3: 2 tests, AC-4: 2 tests, AC-5: 2 tests, AC-6: 2 tests
9 tests fail because stub returns empty list (expected behavior missing), not due to syntax/import/compilation errors
All tests compile and run successfully
Test names and comments clearly indicate what behavior is being tested
Tests use package interview_test and only call exported SelectQuestions function

checklist.item: No implementation code beyond minimal stubs
checklist.details: SelectQuestions is stub returning empty list, no real implementation exists

checklist.item: Tests verify behavior, not just structure
checklist.details: TEST-305 only checks struct is not nil, doesn't verify actual context behavior. Other tests properly verify behavior.
